constraints_1_content: Before writing /app/cache_stats.py, run `cat /app/numbers.txt` in the terminal to inspect the actual file content, line format, and any edge cases (blank lines, trailing spaces) so that the parsing logic is written correctly on the first attempt.
constraints_1_rationale: Inspecting the actual input file before coding is specific to this data-processing task; it references terminal-based inspection rather than any IDE-specific tool.
constraints_2_content: Before running the final script, verify Redis connectivity by executing `redis-cli -h 127.0.0.1 -p 6379 PING` and confirming the response is `PONG`. Only proceed if the check passes.
constraints_2_rationale: This pre-flight connectivity check is specific to the Redis-caching domain; it references shell command interaction in a terminal session.
constraints_3_content: After running the script, validate the stored value and TTL by executing both `redis-cli GET stats:avg` and `redis-cli TTL stats:avg` in the terminal and confirming the outputs are correct before marking the task complete.
constraints_3_rationale: Post-run Redis verification is domain-specific and relies on terminal command execution in the Terminus2Agent environment.
constraints_4_content: If Redis is not running when the script is executed, check whether the `redis-server` binary exists with `which redis-server` before attempting to start it. Do not blindly call `redis-server --daemonize yes` without confirming the binary is available.
constraints_4_rationale: Guarding the daemon-start path with a binary existence check is specific to the Redis infrastructure setup; it involves terminal command sequencing in the Terminus2Agent environment.
